gaps.description: The Bill of Rights exists only as a scaffold — no human principal has authored the Scope of Enterprise, Prohibited Activities, Core Identity, Human Authority Reservation, Amendment Protocol, or Interpretation Rules. The BoR is constitutionally empty, meaning Arbiter has no substantive perimeter to enforce and the MSP has no human intent to translate. The entire governance framework operates in a vacuum.

The MSP seed is active but contains zero operational rules. Nomos evaluates Action Proposals against an empty rule set (default-permissive). There are no machine-actionable objectives, invariants, or safety constraints, because the BoR has not been authored and Senate-Legislator has not published any MSP rules. The compliance gate is open.

No organ-specific or risk-tier-specific genome documents have been created. While logically dependent on a populated MSP, their absence means organs operate without tailored governance controls, even once the MSP is populated. This gap is provisional but architecturally required.

legacy.raw_response:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-BOR-001",
      "reference": "Bill of Rights (BoR) version 1.0.0-seed — all Articles (I–VI) are marked '[To be defined by human principal]'. MSP Purpose states it 'translates the human-authored Bill of Rights into a precise, machine-actionable set of objectives'.",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights exists only as a scaffold — no human principal has authored the Scope of Enterprise, Prohibited Activities, Core Identity, Human Authority Reservation, Amendment Protocol, or Interpretation Rules. The BoR is constitutionally empty, meaning Arbiter has no substantive perimeter to enforce and the MSP has no human intent to translate. The entire governance framework operates in a vacuum.",
      "priority": "critical",
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 5,
      "impact": 5,
      "why": "Without a human-authored BoR, there is no definition of what the organism may or must not do. The oldest and most fundamental gap; every other governance control depends on it."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-MSP-001",
      "reference": "MSP Rules section: 'The bootstrap seed contains no rules. The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation until Senate publishes substantive rules.'",
      "description": "The MSP seed is active but contains zero operational rules. Nomos evaluates Action Proposals against an empty rule set (default-permissive). There are no machine-actionable objectives, invariants, or safety constraints, because the BoR has not been authored and Senate-Legislator has not published any MSP rules. The compliance gate is open.",
      "priority": "critical",
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 5,
      "impact": 5,
      "why": "Coupled with the empty BoR, every Action Proposal passes both Arbiter and Nomos gates without any substantive check. The organism is fully unconstrained."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-GENOME-001",
      "reference": "MSP Genome References section: 'Genome documents live under `genome/` in this directory. They extend the MSP with organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints.' — No genome documents are listed in the seed.",
      "description": "No organ-specific or risk-tier-specific genome documents have been created. While logically dependent on a populated MSP, their absence means organs operate without tailored governance controls, even once the MSP is populated. This gap is provisional but architecturally required.",
      "priority": "low",
      "criticality": 3,
      "urgency": 2,
      "impact": 3,
      "why": "Genome documents are downstream; no immediate risk while the foundational BoR and MSP are incomplete, but they represent a planned extension that will be needed to implement differentiated organ governance."
    }
  ]
}

raw_responses:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "id": "STRAT-GAP-001",
      "title": "Undefined Constitutional Foundation (BoR Unauthored)",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights scaffold remains completely empty (Articles I–VI all marked 'To be defined by human principal'). Without a human-authored BoR, the DIO lacks a constitutional scope, prohibited activities, core identity, and human authority reservation. This prevents the Mission Statement Protocol from translating any strategic intent into machine-actionable objectives, leaving the system with no guiding mission, no compliance perimeter, and no basis for Arbiter enforcement. Memory and context formation specifically have no scope boundaries, no definition of what constitutes relevant knowledge, and no criteria for dream-cycle consolidation priorities. Dream-cycle health cannot be aligned with any strategic goal because none exist.",
      "severity": "critical",
      "impact": "Total strategic paralysis: all governance, safety, scope control, and identity-driven memory management are blocked. The DIO operates in a permissive vacuum with unbounded memory growth and no constitutional checks against mission drift or harmful actions.",
      "affected_msp_section": "Purpose (entire MSP is dependent on a populated BoR)",
      "affected_bor_article": "Articles I (Scope), II (Prohibited Activities), III (Core Identity) – all undefined",
      "priority_score": 125
    },
    {
      "id": "STRAT-GAP-002",
      "title": "Empty Mission Statement Protocol – No Strategic Objectives, OKRs, or Priority Programs",
      "description": "The MSP is a bootstrap scaffold with no rules, no genome references, and no defined objectives, priority programs, or longer-arc goals. Its purpose is to translate the BoR into machine-actionable objectives, yet it contains none. The compliance gate that Nomos evaluates (MSP + genome) is effectively do-nothing, so all Action Proposals pass permissively. For memory and context, there are no quality-of-service targets, retention policies, consolidation cadences, or embedding coverage requirements. This means the Radiant organ and dream-cycle scheduler have no strategic mandate, leading to neglect (scheduled: disabled, last_dream: null). The system cannot evolve its operational constitution because the BoR is also missing.",
      "severity": "critical",
      "impact": "No operational governance. Nomos cannot enforce any policy. Memory and context management drift without strategic alignment, risking catastrophic information loss, clutter, and inability to learn from history. Long-term DIO viability is compromised.",
      "affected_msp_section": "Rules (none), Genome References (none), Purpose (unfulfilled)",
      "affected_bor_article": "Article IV (Human Authority Reservation) – needed to author and maintain the MSP",
      "priority_score": 125
    },
    {
      "id": "STRAT-GAP-003",
      "title": "Dream-Cycle Scheduler Disabled and Health Never Run",
      "description": "The Radiant slice stats show dream-cycle scheduled tasks are disabled, last_dream is null, and health status is 'never_run'. The dream cycle is responsible for context freshness (TTL pruning, promotion) and memory consolidation (clustering, merging). Without it, context blocks are only cleaned up by ad-hoc manual phases; memory consolidation lags, leading to duplicate entries and stale consolidated summaries. The recent low context total (16) and memory bloat (llm-ops 53 entries, avg age 31.8 days) suggest that memory is not being maintained at a sustainable rate. Embedding coverage is only 72%, leaving 27 memory entries without semantic embedding for retrieval. This directly undermines any future strategic objective that would require an accurate, fresh, and self-maintaining knowledge graph.",
      "severity": "high",
      "impact": "Memory integrity decays over time: duplicates survive, old irrelevant memories persist, new facts may be missed in context. Agent decision quality will degrade. Recovery requires a large backfill effort once a constitutional mandate is established.",
      "affected_msp_section": "Genome References (Radiant organ genome would define dream-cycle expectations – currently empty)",
      "affected_bor_article": "Article III (Core Identity) – would define the DIO's self-maintenance mandate",
      "priority_score": 80
    },
    {
      "id": "STRAT-GAP-004",
      "title": "No Organizational Memory Governance Standards",
      "description": "The memory by entity breakdown (llm-ops: 53, coretex: 8, graphheight: 2, others: 1 each) reveals an uneven consolidation pattern. Some entities (e.g., llm-ops) accumulate vast amounts of memory while others are barely represented. There is no MSP rule or genome constraint governing memory consolidation thresholds, entity-level retention policies, or embedding coverage requirements. The 27 memory entries without embeddings represent a blind spot for semantic retrieval. Additionally, the watchdog has never checked dream-cycle health (alert_count: 0, last_check_at: null), indicating that no monitoring or enforcement loop is active.",
      "severity": "medium",
      "impact": "Knowledge graph quality is inconsistent. Critical decisions may rely on incomplete or outdated memory. Over-representation of operational events (backup failures) could bias the system’s understanding, while strategic architectural decisions might be lost if underrepresented entities are neglected.",
      "affected_msp_section": "Genome References (memory lifecycle rules for Radiant) – absent",
      "affected_bor_article": "Article II (Prohibited Activities) – could prevent data hoarding, and Article III (Core Identity) – defines what the DIO must remember about itself",
      "priority_score": 36
    }
  ]
}
